LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RICK MOUSAW'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED AS TO ALL FOUR CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS AN ACTUAL CONTROVERSY BETWEEN THE PARTIES. DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED USE OF THE LINE OF CREDIT THAT WAS SECURED BY PLAINTIFFS' PROPERTY IS A SUFFICIENT CLAIM OR INTEREST IN THE PROPERTY TO SUPPORT A QUIET TITLE CLAIM. PLAINTIFFS MAY PURSUE THEIR ALTERNATIVE CLAIMS FOR INDEMNITY AND UNJUST ENRICHMENT NOW IN THE EVENT THAT THE TRIER OF FACT DETERMINES THAT THE LINE OF CREDIT IS A DEBT ENFORCEABLE AGAINST PLAINTIFFS' PROPERTY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED ORDER OVERULING DEFENDANT RICK MOUSAW'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ALUY, CARMEN CHANG, LUIS A

MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-26-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
(AMENDED) NTC OF MTN AND MTN TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY REPONSES AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS FILED BY DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA N.A EQUITABLE DEED COMPANY

DISCOVERY, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-COMPLAINTS' BANK OF AMERICA AND EQUITABLE DEED COMPANY MOTION TO COMPEL CROSS-DEFENDANT MOUSAW TO MAKE FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NUMBERS 3, 6, 7-27 AND FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1 IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH CROSS-DEFENDANT ASSERTED UNMERITORIOUS OBJECTIONS TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, CROSS-DEFENDANT DENIED EACH OF THEM AND IN HIS RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1 JUSTIFIED THE DENIAL ON THE GROUNDS THAT HE DID NOT HAVE SUFFICIENT RECOLLECTION TO ENABLE HIM TO ADMIT THE ACCURACY OF THE REQUESTED ADMISSION. WHILE ORDINARILY SUCH A BASIS FOR REFUSING TO ADMIT GENERALLY ELICITS A RESPONSE THAT THE PARTY IS UNABLE TO EITHER ADMIT OR DENY, THERE IS NOTHING THAT COMPELS A PARTY TO ACCEPT THE ACCURACY OF A DOCUMENT PREPARED BY THE OPPOSING PARTY AND ADMIT THE FACTS ASSERTED ABSENT AN INDEPENDENT RECOLLECTION OF THE FACTS. HOWEVER, BY FAILING TO ADMIT FACTS WHICH A PARTY IS NOT IN A POSITION TO IN GOOD FAITH CONTEST AND REQUIRING PROOF AT TRIAL, THE PARTY RUNS THE RISK OF SANCTIONS BEING IMPOSED BY REASON OF ITS FAILURE TO SUBMIT EVIDENCE CONTRARY TO THE REQUESTED ADMISSIONS. NO SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED AT THIS TIME. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PROTEM: PAUL RENNE, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-13-2013 Continued To May-06-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-08-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: MARY CATHERINE WIEDERHOLD, ESQ., 415.533.0735, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT RICK MOUSAU; NO OTHER APPEARANCES.

CROSS-COMPLAINANT IS deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.

RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-COMPLAINTS' BANK OF AMERICA AND EQUITABLE DEED COMPANY MOTION TO COMPEL CROSS-DEFENDANT MOUSAW TO MAKE FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NUMBERS 3, 6, 7-27 AND FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1 IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH CROSS-DEFENDANT ASSERTED UNMERITORIOUS OBJECTIONS TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, CROSS-DEFENDANT DENIED EACH OF THEM AND IN HIS RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1 JUSTIFIED THE DENIAL ON THE GROUNDS THAT HE DID NOT HAVE SUFFICIENT RECOLLECTION TO ENABLE HIM TO ADMIT THE ACCURACY OF THE REQUESTED ADMISSION. WHILE ORDINARILY SUCH A BASIS FOR REFUSING TO ADMIT GENERALLY ELICITS A RESPONSE THAT THE PARTY IS UNABLE TO EITHER ADMIT OR DENY, THERE IS NOTHING THAT COMPELS A PARTY TO ACCEPT THE ACCURACY OF A DOCUMENT PREPARED BY THE OPPOSING PARTY AND ADMIT THE FACTS ASSERTED ABSENT AN INDEPENDENT RECOLLECTION OF THE FACTS. HOWEVER, BY FAILING TO ADMIT FACTS WHICH A PARTY IS NOT IN A POSITION TO IN GOOD FAITH CONTEST AND REQUIRING PROOF AT TRIAL, THE PARTY RUNS THE RISK OF SANCTIONS BEING IMPOSED BY REASON OF ITS FAILURE TO SUBMIT EVIDENCE CONTRARY TO THE REQUESTED ADMISSIONS. NO SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED AT THIS TIME. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge ProTem: PAUL RENNE, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-15-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERT W. BIEDERMAN OF THE STEYER LOWENTHAL & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 421-3400 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT RICK MOUSAW'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled as to all four causes of action. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads an actual controversy between the parties. Defendant's alleged use of the line of credit that was secured by plaintiffs' property is a sufficient claim or interest in the property to support a quiet title claim. Plaintiffs may pursue their alternative claims for indemnity and unjust enrichment now in the event that the trier of fact determines that the line of credit is a debt enforceable against plaintiffs' property. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/HK
